assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the summary table shows 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. The detailed Correctness Findings section lists all five criteria individually as PASS: (1) Single license filter, (2) Comma-separated license filter returns union, (3) Invalid license returns 400 Bad Request, (4) Filter integrates with existing pagination, (5) Response shape unchanged. Each criterion file (criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md) also marks its result as PASS.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the summary table shows 'Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 files in diff match Files to Modify/Create in task spec exactly'. The Intent Alignment Findings section contains a file-by-file comparison table listing: Modify: modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs (Present in diff), Modify: modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (Present in diff), Create: tests/api/package.rs (Present in diff). It also states 'No files outside the task specification were touched. Scope is perfectly contained.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the summary table shows 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created'. There is no mention of sub-task creation anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs consist entirely of report files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md) which are read-only verification documents. The report discusses reading and analyzing the diff but never mentions editing, modifying, or writing to any source code files (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs). All output files contain analysis and findings only.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and presents a summary table with findings. There is no mention of merging, approving, or auto-merging the PR anywhere in any output file. The report presents its findings (verdicts, evidence, and detailed analysis) for human review without taking any merge action.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the summary table shows 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No passwords, API keys, tokens, or private keys detected'. The Security Findings section provides detailed line-level scanning results: 'Hardcoded credentials: None found. No passwords, API keys, tokens, or bearer strings.' 'Private keys: No PEM blocks or key material.' 'Environment secrets: No .env references, no std::env::var calls for secrets.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the summary table shows 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file with 4 tests; no existing tests modified or removed'. The Style/Conventions Findings section elaborates: 'Test Change Classification: ADDITIVE - tests/api/package.rs is a newly created file (index 0000000..a1b2c3d). No existing test files were modified or removed. All changes are purely additive test coverage.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md summary table contains all expected verdicts: Intent Alignment domain: Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (PASS). Security domain: Sensitive Patterns (PASS). Correctness domain: CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Verification Commands (N/A). Style/Conventions domain: Test Quality (PASS, with note 'Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews)') and Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE). The detailed findings sections are organized by domain: Intent Alignment Findings, Security Findings, Correctness Findings, and Style/Conventions Findings.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment: report.md includes a file-by-file comparison table mapping task spec files to diff files with status, plus narrative about scope containment and diff size proportionality. Security: Detailed line-level scan covering hardcoded credentials, private keys, environment secrets, SQL injection analysis (noting SeaORM parameterized queries), and input validation (noting spdx::Expression::parse). Correctness: Five per-criterion sections each with code-level evidence showing implementation details, query construction, and test assertions. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide extensive code-level evidence. Style/Conventions: Detailed test quality assessment covering test coverage, pattern conformance, naming conventions, repetitive logic detection, and file location. All four domains have substantive findings beyond the summary table.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the Test Quality row states 'PASS | 4 tests cover all 4 test requirements; Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews)'. The Style/Conventions Findings section ends with 'Eval Quality: N/A -- No eval result reviews present.' This confirms Eval Quality is N/A and does not affect the Test Quality verdict.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A as shown in report.md ('Eval Quality: N/A -- No eval result reviews present.'). There is no mention of eval failure sub-tasks being created anywhere in the output files. The Root-Cause Investigation row shows 'N/A | No sub-tasks created', confirming no sub-tasks of any kind were created.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the summary table lists 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created' as the first two rows, appearing before the domain-specific verdicts (Scope Containment, Diff Size, etc.). These verdicts are not discussed in any of the four domain findings sections (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, Style/Conventions), confirming they are determined independently from domain analysis.
